name: HEC Need-Based Scholarship Program
criteria: Pakistani nationals with family income below HEC's threshold, enrolled in HEC-recognized universities.
deadlineWindow: Opens ~August/September each year — confirm on official site
description: Covers tuition, books, and a monthly stipend for undergraduates from low-income households.

name: Punjab Educational Endowment Fund (PEEF)
criteria: Domiciled in Punjab, merit + financial need based, family income below PEEF threshold.
deadlineWindow: Varies by cycle — check PEEF portal
description: Merit-cum-need scholarship for Punjab-domiciled students at intermediate and undergraduate levels.

name: Ehsaas Undergraduate Scholarship Program
criteria: Financially disadvantaged Pakistani undergraduates, allocation weighted toward underdeveloped districts and women.
deadlineWindow: Annual cycle — confirm on HEC portal
description: Nationwide need-based scholarship prioritizing students from underserved regions.

name: Sindh Endowment Fund Scholarship
criteria: Domiciled in Sindh, merit and need-based, studying at recognized institutions in Pakistan.
deadlineWindow: Varies by cycle — check SEF portal
description: Supports Sindh-domiciled students pursuing undergraduate or graduate studies.

name: IBA Karachi Need-Based Financial Assistance
criteria: Admitted IBA students demonstrating financial need via documentation.
description: Institution-specific financial aid for admitted IBA students who can't afford full fees.

name: LUMS National Outreach Programme (NOP)
criteria: Talented students from under-resourced schools/districts, financial need based.
description: Full-ride program for high-potential students who otherwise couldn't access LUMS.

name: Fulbright Scholarship (Pakistan)
criteria: Pakistani nationals with a bachelor's degree, competitive academic record, for master's/PhD study in the US.
deadlineWindow: Typically opens ~March, closes ~May — confirm on USEFP site
description: Fully funded graduate study in the United States for Pakistani professionals and students.

name: Chevening Scholarship
criteria: 2+ years of work experience, leadership potential, applying for a one-year master's in the UK.
deadlineWindow: Typically opens ~August, closes ~November — confirm on official site
description: UK government's flagship global scholarship for future leaders pursuing a UK master's degree.

name: Commonwealth Shared Scholarship
criteria: Citizens of eligible Commonwealth developing countries, cannot afford UK study independently.
deadlineWindow: Typically closes ~December/January — confirm on official site
description: Funds master's study in the UK in subjects linked to development priorities.

name: DAAD Scholarship (Germany)
criteria: Bachelor's degree with strong academic record; program-specific requirements vary.
deadlineWindow: Varies by program — usually Oct/Nov for the following year
description: Wide portfolio of funded master's and PhD programs in Germany across most fields.

name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master's Scholarship
criteria: Bachelor's degree, admission to a participating joint master's program across 2+ EU countries.
description: Study a joint master's across multiple European universities, fully funded.

name: Gates Cambridge Scholarship
criteria: Outstanding academic record, admitted to a full-time postgraduate course at Cambridge, non-UK citizen.
description: Highly competitive full scholarship for postgraduate study at Cambridge.

name: Rhodes Scholarship
criteria: Exceptional academic and leadership record, aged roughly 18–24, applying for postgraduate study at Oxford.
deadlineWindow: Varies by country constituency — usually mid-year
description: One of the oldest and most prestigious international postgraduate scholarships.

name: Schwarzman Scholars
field: Leadership, Business, Public Policy, International Studies
criteria: Bachelor's degree, strong leadership potential, one-year master's in Global Affairs at Tsinghua.
description: Fully funded one-year master's in China for future global leaders.

name: Knight-Hennessy Scholars
criteria: Admitted or applying to any Stanford graduate program, strong leadership and academic record.
description: Full funding to pursue any graduate degree at Stanford as part of a leadership cohort.

name: NUST Merit & Need-Based Scholarship
criteria: Admitted NUST students with strong academic merit or demonstrated financial need.
description: Tuition support for high-achieving or financially constrained NUST undergraduates.

name: Habib University Financial Aid Program
criteria: Admitted students with demonstrated financial need, need-blind admission policy.
description: Need-based aid designed so admitted students can attend regardless of financial background.

name: USAID Merit and Needs-Based Scholarship Program
criteria: Pakistani students from underserved areas, especially women and minorities, at partner universities.
deadlineWindow: Aligned with partner university admission cycles
description: Targets underrepresented Pakistani students at select partner universities across STEM and business fields.

name: Aga Khan Foundation International Scholarship
criteria: Outstanding students from select developing countries with no other means to finance a master's/PhD.
description: Combination grant-and-loan funding for postgraduate study for students with no alternative funding.

name: Google Generation Scholarship
criteria: Studying Computer Science / related field, demonstrated leadership in supporting underrepresented groups in tech.
description: Award for CS students who champion diversity and inclusion in computing.

name: Adobe Research Women-in-Technology Scholarship
criteria: Female-identifying students in CS/Engineering with strong academic record.
description: Supports women pursuing degrees in computing and engineering fields.

name: Orange Tulip Scholarship (Pakistan)
criteria: Pakistani nationals admitted to a partner Dutch university/program.
description: Tuition support for Pakistani students admitted to partner institutions in the Netherlands.

name: Türkiye Bursları (Türkiye Scholarships)
criteria: International applicants meeting age and academic thresholds per degree level.
description: Comprehensive fully funded scholarship covering all degree levels in Türkiye.

name: MCM Scholarship for Postgraduate Studies (Pakistan)
provider: M. Anwar and Amna Inayatullah Trust
criteria: Pakistani students admitted to a recognized postgraduate program with demonstrated need.
deadlineWindow: Varies by cycle — check trust listings
description: Local trust-funded support for Pakistani postgraduate students with financial need.
